content.claim: Act CXC of 2011 on National Public Education establishes the statutory basis for Hungary's 6- and 8-year gimnázium tracks as upper-secondary programmes within grades 5–12 or 7–12, with entrance examinations centrally organized but operating within a statutory regime of free school choice for all students.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: The statute itself does not explicitly justify early selection on pedagogical grounds, only permits it
Free-choice guarantee and centralized exam gatekeeping create an internal legal tension the statute does not resolve
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
